briefing.title: Margin briefing · auto-drafted, editable · audit-ready

paragraphs.html: Subject: Margin position by cluster · YTD Apr 2026
YTD margin 24.1%, 3.9pp below plan (industry typical 27–30% for VDMA precision pumps). Total leakage −€187K across 5,565 invoices.
Margin by cluster: BKAES 25% (target 28%, conf 82%) · BKAGG 21% (target 26%, conf 74%) · BKAIZ 18% (low-n, conf 64%) · SOPU 14% (⚠ low-n, conf 38%, do not auto-act).
Movable lens: Of the −€187K YTD leakage, ~€116K (62%) sits on movable revenue and is actionable this cycle. ~€71K (38%) is locked under frame contracts and belongs in Till's renegotiation queue.

Three buckets account for 92% of the leak: cost not passed through €150K (7 of 18 indexed contracts un-triggered — 4 movable, 3 locked), discounting €117K (47 below-guardrail breaches across 12 reps — 78% concentrated in 3 reps; Heiko coordinating), customer-mix shift €64K (more tier-C/D revenue, less tier-A growth).

Lost-quote margin differential: Won quotes carried 70.6% margin vs 72.4% on lost (diff +1.8pp, p=0.006, n=1,313). The lost-quote margin is higher than won — we lose disproportionately on the high-margin end. Different problem from the leakage waterfall, different fix (qualification/negotiation, not pass-through). Shared with Heiko in Deal Empowerment.

What shifted vs last month: Cost-not-passed-through +€18K (steel +1.6pp WoW), Discounting flat, Cross-customer-spread −€4K (one fix shipped after 21-day A/B confirmed +2.4pp lift, p=0.018). Net −€14K worse.

Recovery via Pryzm: €147K captured YTD across 38 implemented actions — all hash-signed, 12 went through A/B tracker before broad rollout. Closable gap in the pipeline: €280K (€180K within my authority · €100K needs Till's sign-off).

briefing.signature: — Frank, Pricing Analyst / Head of Controlling · drafted by Pryzm · please review before forwarding to Till.

health.label: Margin health score
health.sub: −4 vs last month · 3.9pp gap to plan

shifted.netLine: Net month-over-month: −€14K worse · driven by steel pass-through gap.

waterfall.title: Where the 3.9pp gap came from
waterfall.subtitle: Every PP of margin gap mapped to a cause bucket. Three account for 92% of the leak.
infoPanel: Every PP of margin gap is mapped to a cause bucket. Three buckets account for 92% of the leak.
Each bucket is a doorway — click any bucket to drill into the actionable view (sales-rep breaches, contract pass-through, segment pivot, or list erosion).

buckets.name: Customer mix shift
buckets.source: More tier-C/D revenue · less tier-A growth · cluster mix BKAES/BKAGG conf 78%

buckets.name: Discounting (sales below guardrail)
buckets.source: 47 breaches Q1 · 12 reps · top 3 = 78% · cluster spread BKAES 82%, BKAGG 74%

buckets.name: Cost not passed through
buckets.source: 7 of 18 indexed contracts un-triggered · 4 movable, 3 locked · cluster mix BKAES/BKAGG conf 78%

buckets.name: Rebate over-accrual
buckets.source: 3 customers above committed tier · cluster BKAGG conf 74%

buckets.name: List-price erosion
buckets.source: 11mo avg list lag across 24 SKUs · cluster BKAES 82% · BKAIZ 64%

movableLocked.source: Pilot estimate · derived from price_governance.price_rules + frame-contract dates

lostQuote.title: Lost-quote margin differential — why we lose at the high-margin end
lostQuote.subtitle: The lost-quote average margin is higher than the won-quote average. Different fix from leakage waterfall.
lostQuote.interpretationHtml: Plain-language interpretation: The lost-quote average margin is higher than the won-quote average. We are losing disproportionately on the high-margin end — the pricing model is leaving high-margin business on the table. This is a different problem than the leakage waterfall above and needs a different fix: qualification + negotiation arguments + price-anchor coaching, not pass-through.
lostQuote.sourceHtml: Source · pricing_analysis.price_sensitivity · Shared with Heiko (Sales) — appears in Deal Empowerment as lost-deal analytics. Same finding shared with Till in monthly briefing.

costVsPrice.title: Input cost vs realized price · last 24 months
costVsPrice.subtitle: When the cost line rises faster than the price line, your margin is compressing — visible here before it shows up in the P&L.
infoPanel: When the cost line rises faster than the price line, your margin is compressing — visible here before it shows up in the P&L.
Forward 12-mo cost trajectory feeds from Forecast → Input cost trajectory. This view is the trailing 24-mo backward look.

passThrough.label: Cost pass-through completion
passThrough.sub: 11 of 18 indexed contracts triggered YTD · 7 un-triggered = €150K leakage
passThrough.breakdownHtml: Of the 7 un-triggered: 4 movable (Frank can renegotiate index trigger this cycle) · 3 locked (Till to take to MD review at frame renewal).

recovery.label: YTD margin recovery via Pryzm
recovery.sub: From 38 implemented actions · cumulative · 12 went through A/B tracker before broad rollout · all hash-signed

cross.description: Same article, same volume tier, different customers — sorted by spread.
infoPanel: This is the data layer no Excel can build. Cross-checks 5,565 invoices against 4,605 quotes, normalizes for tier and quarter, flags pairs where the spread can't be justified by volume or contract terms.
cross.footerNote: Cluster note: Spreads in low-n clusters (SOPU n=6, conf 38%) are flagged ⚠ — review manually before raising in Studio. None in this view.

leak.description: SKU-level margin leakage · quoted vs invoiced.
infoPanel: Feeds the Discounting bucket in the waterfall above (€117K of €417K total leakage). The gap between quoted margin and actual margin = price erosion at invoice time.
leak.tabFooterText: 11 actions / 90d · 6 implemented (all hash-signed) · 4 went through A/B before broad rollout · €44,200 captured YTD

seg.description: Slice along 4 dimensions to find where the leak concentrates.
infoPanel: Each cut surfaces a different fix: family-level pricing reset, tier-level renegotiation, deal-size guardrail tightening, or regional rep coaching.
Feeds the Customer mix shift bucket in the waterfall above (€64K).

subPanes.label: By product family
subPanes.storyHtml: Story: Precision shafts (Article 200832-E family) are the biggest single drag — 74% of total margin gap concentrated here. Sleeves are healthy.

subPanes.label: By customer tier

rows.label: Volume
cells: 101582 above plan — possible to monetize further

rows.label: Problematic
cells: 101900 — raise price even at attrition risk

subPanes.storyHtml: Story: Strategic tier is leaking 4pp because of one customer (101580). Problematic tier should be repriced — 11pp gap is structural, not relationship-driven.

subPanes.label: By deal size
subPanes.storyHtml: Story: Counterintuitively, large deals leak the most (4pp gap). Sales discounting concentrates on big-customer negotiations — exactly where guardrails should bite hardest.

subPanes.label: By region
subPanes.storyHtml: Story: BKAGG is the single biggest regional drag (−7pp · €115K leakage). The Monday Briefing already flagged this — sales-rep level intervention needed, not a pricing change.
subPanes.caveatHtml: ⚠ Naming caveat (Frank's note): "BKAGG" here is a regional sales label — not the BKAGG commodity cluster. The two share the abbreviation by historical accident. Disambiguation pending in the next data-model update; treat them as distinct dimensions.

erode.description: Stale list prices · cost vs list movement.
infoPanel: List prices erode silently when cost moves up faster than the price book gets refreshed. The September price-book question, answered every Monday.
Avg list-update lag across portfolio: 11 months (industry best-practice: 6 months). Feeds List-price erosion bucket · €32K.
erode.cycleNote: Cycle output: Triggering the price-book cycle auto-generates a Branded PDF (Scherzinger corporate design) with all 24 SKUs · audit-hash signed · routed to Till for sign-off.
erode.tabFooterText: Feeds List-price erosion bucket · €32K · 24 SKUs > 9 months stale

infoPanel: Status pill flags accounts needing intervention vs. healthy ones. Trend shows YoY margin movement (12-mo rolling).
Use the Drill button on any row to open the per-month trajectory + cohort comparison; Open action routes the customer into your weekly queue.
